text.title: Requirement Quality Scorecard — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `requirement-quality-scorecard` must enforce. The rules pin the discipline that turns a scorecard from folklore into a reviewable, owned, version-controlled operating tool.

rule.statement: The scorecard has 4–7 named dimensions, each with an explicit 1–5 anchor description per score. 'Subjective overall' is forbidden as a dimension.

rationale.source: Behaviourally Anchored Rating Scale (BARS) literature; structured-interview research

rule.rationale: Without anchored scales the same artefact scores 3 from one reviewer and 5 from another. Anchored 1–5 scales make inter-rater agreement measurable.

rule.statement: Each score carries one verbatim evidence pointer (quote, line, screenshot, metric). Scores without evidence are invalid and re-graded.
rule.rationale: Unanchored scoring degrades to opinion. Tying score to evidence makes reviews defensible and audit-ready.

rule.statement: Aggregate score is computed by a published rule (min, weighted sum, or veto-on-any-1). 'Reviewer's overall feeling' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Aggregation hides poor dimensions when reviewers compensate. A published rule makes the verdict reproducible and challengeable.

rule.statement: Before the scorecard goes live, ≥2 reviewers score the same 5 artefacts and reconcile differences ≥ 2 points. Disagreements update the anchor descriptions.

rationale.source: Inter-rater reliability methodology; assessment-development best practice

rule.rationale: First-use of a scorecard surfaces ambiguous anchors. The calibration pass fixes them before the scorecard is used on real artefacts that matter.

rule.statement: The scorecard carries `version` + `last_calibrated_at` + named owner. Stale calibration auto-expires after 6 months.

rationale.source: ISO 9001 document control; structured-assessment maintenance practice

rule.rationale: BAs need a deterministic scorecard (atomicity, testability, traceability, value, ambiguity, completeness) they can run on every requirement before baseline. Today the corpus discusses these as principles inside prioritization / validation but provides no scoring artefact. P4 BAs handing to foreign clients need an objective quality signal.
